reviews.review_title: They promised us a free quest edition
reviews.review_description: But instead they made a subscription model and forced us to pay again. Very disappointing behaviour.

I purchased Gear VR version, how and when can I use it on oculus quest?
Thank!

developer_response.body: Hello xiaomand. We have a Quest version available, just search for Human Anatomy VR in AppLab :).

reviews.review_description: I purchased this app for the Oculus Go, and when I learned about the free update I purchased the Oculus Quest. Now they are offering a subscription model. This has to be illegal. To make a comment to the consumer and then go contrary to it.

This app really helped my spatial understanding on how the organs relate to each other. The quiz feature gives it some potential use as a study tool as well.
I would love it if the developers could bring this app to the Oculus Quest, so I can lean into the body and such, looking forward to any future updates!

developer_response.body:
Hi jamman,
Thank you for your review! Our native app for Oculus Quest is ready and awaiting approval to be published, hopefully it will be available as soon as possible.

reviews.review_description: I bought this app a few years ago for my oculus go. Then its support was extended to the oculus quest. However, It only allows you to use the demo unless you buy it again. I contacted the developers regarding this issue but they never answered.

developer_response.body:
Hi tomatecanario,
We are sorry for your inconvenience, our Complete Edition is not currently available for Oculus Quest due to the fact it is awaiting approval to be published.

reviews.review_description: Do you guys have an update to this app?

reviews.review_title: Too rough to be worth the money
reviews.review_description:
Frankly, I feel that a mobile anatomy app or a book are better than this. I don't find reading text in VR very comfortable, and user interface feels basic, selection and rotation don't produce the result I desire (I don't at all see the point of upside down rotation). The models feel rough and I often felt that a book showed me more details.

In short, it's a nice idea and has a bit of attraction, but I feel that it's too basic to be worth the price, even though I bought it at only $10. I would have liked something that gives me a lot more information about how various body systems work, in a graphical and more immersive manner.

developer_response.body:
Hi ET3D,
We are sorry that you are not pleased with our application. But, we are also limited by the performance of the devices in VR. We are trying to improve our application and update our models as much as possible.

reviews.review_description: No longer launches on Oculus Go.

I like this app for teaching, but I cannot find the icon for text to speech  on the left side of the text or anywhere? Where is it? How I can get the text to speech icon very frustrating that I can only have text with the labels!

developer_response.body: Hi morigua, I am very sorry, but text-to-speech is available only for Samsung Gear VR devices, because Oculus Go doesn't support it yet. We will release an update for Oculus Go as soon as it is available.

reviews.review_description: Need to have the info spoken to you rather than just read

developer_response.body:
Hello RobvBarton,
I am not sure if I understand you. We have a function text-to-speech, just simply click on the icon located on the left side of the info box.

reviews.review_description:
The application is great, both for adults and teenagers, it is great to remember and learn the structure of the human body. Just start it, we see a menu, in which we can choose a tutorial that will teach us the management to consult the information of each part of the human body. The interface is very intuitive, and the management is very simple.
We choose what we want to observe, be it skelete system, nervous system, respiratory system, muscular system, connective tissues, arteries, reproductive system, lymphatic system, etc ...!
We have a text and spoken description of each part of the body that we observe (I suggest the developers also Spanish language).
We press with the controller or touchpad for example in the muscular system, and then the part of the body that we want to see in the skeleton. We will expand to see it better, we can also use zoom in or out and as I commented previously see the description and the name if we want in Latin. We can also make swipe with the touchpad rotate the body part as we want to see it from different angles, and a button to center the image to its original state.

We can make multiple selections of body parts, ie mix for example muscular system and connective tissues.
Another very useful function that the application has is the search engine, we can quickly find the part of the body that we want.
It also has details such as changing the background, empty space, or universe.

The graphics are very good, they look very sharp and with good resolution, it requires a good Galaxy phone, I have tested it on a Galaxy S8 and I have not gotten the warming warning, but it has warmed up a lot

The summary is that we have a very complete educational application to learn and improve the knowledge of many parts of the human body and that will receive updates to improve it. Only the price is a bit expensive but it is worth it. Totally recommended.

Looking forward to more content, like more detail about the brain and heart. More depth, plus narrated descriptions and body worlds imagery would be welcome. Good start though. Glad I waited for the daily deal.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jeffo2019, 
we are constantly improving the content of anatomy systems. In the latest update we have added very detailed anatomy model of brain. In the next updates we will prepare detailed model of heart and etc. If you mean some specific anatomy structure please write us to mail info@medicinevirtual.com

reviews.review_title: Why should I buy this program again?
reviews.review_description:
I bought whole version last year for my samsung gear VR.
I could use it with my oculus quest, but there are prices...why if I already bought it?

developer_response.body:
Hi szabozr,
Thats due to the fact that Oculus Quest is only showing the non Complete version that is a freemium version. Unfortunately, our Complete Edition is not currently available on the Oculus Quest and is awaiting approval to be published. Sorry for your inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: Good

You must update the system to include the full female anatomy... It's hard to get females involved, and interested in the app, when the representation of them is just of the genital region... They have a brain... Thank you...

developer_response.body:
Hi MRBARKS, 

We are planning to update the full anatomy of female in a near future. Due to the fact, that it takes a lot of time to and work to create it. We decided to start with the actual version  that shows the main differences between the female and the male.

reviews.review_title: The App is buggy on quest 2
reviews.review_description: I first purchased the Anatomy App for my Gear VR and 3years later i bought my 2 device specifically because the app was that good but unfortunately to my chagrin, i have not been able to get the app on my quest 2 since despite i was told i could migrate my license, very sad indeed. :-(

reviews.review_title: Decent for helping visualising in 3D but
reviews.review_description:
I'm a little disappointed for the price, bought this to use with students. the models are good and the functions you can use to look around are good. The detail in how everything has all the text descriptions is okay but it could do with having animations for showing the different functions of the main systems. 
Like the blood flow of the heart and lungs. 
More models for looking at the smaller scale processes, like oxygenating blood in the lungs, digesting food with water and nutrients etc into the blood stream. 
Hopefully it will receive updates but unfortunately for the price i cant justify it. As it is now its an encyclopedia but you can look at 3d models, so students cant really learn how the body functions any better than reading text, they just learn where things are.

developer_response.body:
Hi GraemeEvans,
Our application is focusing on Anatomy, the features that you are talking about are about Physiology. But, we are planning to add these features in the future.

reviews.review_description: I’m a first year nursing student and it doesn’t have half the stuff I’ve learned in the first 6 weeks of my class. Shocking that this can be sold as the complete edition! Nothing complete about it. Also the annoying telephone music played constantly!

developer_response.body:
Hi bebebo50,
We are sorry to hear that you did not like our application and found it lacking with information. Could you please contact us at info@medicinevirtual.com and tell us more about what you were missing? Thanks!

reviews.review_description: I purchased it for the quest but I can only get the demo working. It was advertised as if it would work on quest too. I contacted developers. No answer. This was months ago, close to a year. Please resolve.

developer_response.body:
Hello raggao,
The complete edition of our application is not available for the Oculus Quest unfortunately and is currently awaiting approval to be published.

reviews.review_description: The tutorial does not function.  You get to the four part and it will not allow you to perform the action of highlighting a part.  It tells you to double click to perform an earlier function but the single click performs the function.   When it tells you to single click to do something the click does nothing.   The sound option to tell you about parts does not work.    There is no sound at all in this crappy APP even though there are a settings for sound and music.   I would not waste my money on this worthless unfinished software.

developer_response.body: Hi, We are sorry to hear you had a bad experience with our product. We will fix the misleading step in the tutorial in the upcoming update. (In the tutorial, you have to tap on the touchpad instead of clicking). The function "text to speech" is working properly as far as we know. Can you tell us on what device are you using our application? And you can switch on the sound in Menu – Setting – Sound. Our product provides you with both anatomical structures in 3D and the necessary information from the anatomical textbooks, what in result is cheaper than the printed textbooks.
